questions.text: A recruiter spends approximately how long on an initial CV scan?
questions.explanation: Recruiters scan a CV in seconds. A 1-page, keyword-rich format ensures you survive the scan.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

Recruiters scan a CV in seconds. A 1-page, keyword-rich format ensures you survive the scan.

questions.text: Which of the following is the strongest line to include on a Web3 CV?

options.text: “Deployed a staking contract on MST Testnet, reducing gas per stake from 120k to 40k - 0xABCD”

questions.explanation: Measurable achievement + contract address is real proof. All other options are vague claims.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

Measurable achievement + contract address is real proof. All other options are vague claims.

questions.text: How many repos should you pin on your GitHub profile for maximum impact?
questions.explanation: 3 - 5 pinned repos give enough breadth without diluting focus. Each must have a thorough README.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: C

3 - 5 pinned repos give enough breadth without diluting focus. Each must have a thorough README.

questions.text: What is the most convincing proof of smart contract development skills?

options.text: A certificate from an online course
A verified contract on a block explorer with transaction history

questions.explanation: Verifiable on-chain code is irrefutable proof. Certificates and recommendations are secondary.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: C

Verifiable on-chain code is irrefutable proof. Certificates and recommendations are secondary.

questions.text: Which of these is the LEAST effective way to showcase Web3 expertise?

options.text: Writing a technical blog post about gas optimisation
Listing 15 blockchain courses on your CV without any projects
Contributing to an open-source protocol repository
Presenting a dApp demo at a local meetup

questions.explanation: Courses without projects signal consumption, not creation. All other options demonstrate active contribution.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

Courses without projects signal consumption, not creation. All other options demonstrate active contribution.

questions.text: “A PDF CV is the most important part of a Web3 developer's portfolio.”
questions.explanation: 1 mark: Correct verdict (FALSE). +1 mark: Clear justification mentioning that GitHub repos and on-chain verification are the real proof, not the PDF.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Breakdown

1 mark: Correct verdict (FALSE). +1 mark: Clear justification mentioning that GitHub repos and on-chain verification are the real proof, not the PDF.

questions.text: “Listing ‘Solidity’ on your CV is sufficient proof of smart contract development ability.”
questions.explanation: 1 mark: FALSE. +1 mark: Justification explains the need for verifiable proof (GitHub link, contract address, etc.).
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Breakdown

1 mark: FALSE. +1 mark: Justification explains the need for verifiable proof (GitHub link, contract address, etc.).

questions.text: You are applying for a Smart Contract Engineer role at an MST-based DeFi startup. Describe exactly what you would include in your CV's “Projects” section and explain why each element matters. Provide a sample bullet point for one of your own (real or imagined) projects.
questions.explanation:
A strong answer includes:
        • At least 3 project entries, each with: project name, your role, tech stack, a measurable achievement, and a link to GitHub/explorer.
        • Explanation that on-chain verification and gas optimisation metrics are key differentiators.
        • A sample bullet like: “Gas-Optimised Staking Pool - Wrote Solidity contract using OpenZeppelin and Hardhat; reduced gas per stake from 150k to 45k via storage packing; 100% test coverage; deployed & verified on MST Testnet (0x1234…).”
        Marks: 1 mark for structure, 1 mark for explaining why metrics/links matter, 1 mark for a high-quality sample bullet.
questions.modelAnswer:
📝 Model Answer (scoring guide)

A strong answer includes:
• At least 3 project entries, each with: project name, your role, tech stack, a measurable achievement, and a link to GitHub/explorer.
• Explanation that on-chain verification and gas optimisation metrics are key differentiators.
• A sample bullet like: “Gas-Optimised Staking Pool - Wrote Solidity contract using OpenZeppelin and Hardhat; reduced gas per stake from 150k to 45k via storage packing; 100% test coverage; deployed & verified on MST Testnet (0x1234…).”

Marks: 1 mark for structure, 1 mark for explaining why metrics/links matter, 1 mark for a high-quality sample bullet.

questions.text: Submit a single PDF containing: (a) Your updated 1-page Web3 CV, (b) A screenshot of your GitHub profile with at least 3 pinned repositories, and (c) A link to one MST testnet contract you have deployed and verified. The PDF must be named YourName_Portfolio_20.2.pdf.
questions.explanation:
1 mark: CV is 1 page, includes GitHub and explorer links, and avoids generic phrases.
        1 mark: GitHub screenshot shows 3+ pinned repos with READMEs.
        1 mark: Provided MST testnet contract address is verified and matches the CV claim.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Breakdown

1 mark: CV is 1 page, includes GitHub and explorer links, and avoids generic phrases.
1 mark: GitHub screenshot shows 3+ pinned repos with READMEs.
1 mark: Provided MST testnet contract address is verified and matches the CV claim.
